title: cicuta graeca
intro: Cicuta graeca, also known as Greek water hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Despite its toxic nature, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Cicuta graeca include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and poultices, but it is essential to exercise extreme caution when handling it due to its high toxicity.
